revision.text:
{{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] [[Bodhisattva]] in Tushita Heaven. [[Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[Nalanda]], [[Bihar]], [[India]]]]
'''Tuṣita''' ([[Sanskrit]]) or '''Tusita''' ([[Pāli]]) is one of the six [[Deva (Buddhism)|deva]]-worlds of the [[Desire realm|Desire Realm]] (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[meditation]]. It is the heaven where the [[Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[gana]] deities: [[adityas]], [[visvedevas]], [[vasus]], tushitas, [[abhasvaras]], anilas, [[maharajikas]], [[Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitabha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.
== References == {{commonscat|Tushita heaven}} <references/>

Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitabha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

{{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] [[Bodhisattva]] in Tushita Heaven. [[Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[Nalanda]], [[Bihar]], [[India]]]]
'''Tuṣita''' ([[Sanskrit]]) or '''Tusita''' ([[Pāli]]) is one of the six [[Deva (Buddhism)|deva]]-worlds of the [[Desire realm|Desire Realm]] (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[meditation]]. It is the heaven where the [[Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[gana]] deities: [[adityas]], [[visvedevas]], [[vasus]], tushitas, [[abhasvaras]], anilas, [[maharajikas]], [[Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. == Descriptions ==

{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitabha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitabha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

revision.text:
{{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] [[Bodhisattva]] in Tushita Heaven. [[Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[Nalanda]], [[Bihar]], [[India]]]]
'''Tuṣita''' ([[Sanskrit]]) or '''Tusita''' ([[Pāli]]) is one of the six [[Deva (Buddhism)|deva]]-worlds of the [[Desire realm|Desire Realm]] (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[meditation]]. It is the heaven where the [[Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[gana]] deities: [[adityas]], [[visvedevas]], [[vasus]], tushitas, [[abhasvaras]], anilas, [[maharajikas]], [[Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitabha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

{{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] [[Bodhisattva]] in Tushita Heaven. [[Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[Nalanda]], [[Bihar]], [[India]]]]
'''Tuṣita''' ([[Sanskrit]]) or '''Tusita''' ([[Pāli]]) is one of the six [[Deva (Buddhism)|deva]]-worlds of the [[Desire realm|Desire Realm]] (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[meditation]]. It is the heaven where the [[Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[gana]] deities: [[adityas]], [[visvedevas]], [[vasus]], tushitas, [[abhasvaras]], anilas, [[maharajikas]], [[Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitabha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}

Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[gana]] deities: [[adityas]], [[visvedevas]], [[vasus]], tushitas, [[abhasvaras]], anilas, [[maharajikas]], [[Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitabha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

Most Buddhist literature holds that [[Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[gana]] deities: [[adityas]], [[visvedevas]], [[vasus]], tushitas, [[abhasvaras]], anilas, [[maharajikas]], [[Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}

In [[Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[gana]] deities: [[adityas]], [[visvedevas]], [[vasus]], tushitas, [[abhasvaras]], anilas, [[maharajikas]], [[Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitabha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

'''Tuṣita''' ([[Sanskrit]]) or '''Tusita''' ([[Pāli]]) is one of the six [[Deva (Buddhism)|deva]]-worlds of the [[Desire realm|Desire Realm]] (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. It is the heaven where the [[Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[gana]] deities: [[adityas]], [[visvedevas]], [[vasus]], tushitas, [[abhasvaras]], anilas, [[maharajikas]], [[Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitabha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

In [[Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[gana]] deities: [[adityas]], [[visvedevas]], [[vasus]], tushitas, [[abhasvaras]], anilas, [[maharajikas]], [[Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitabha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[meditation]]. It is the heaven where the [[Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>

In [[Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[gana]] deities: [[adityas]], [[visvedevas]], [[vasus]], tushitas, [[abhasvaras]], anilas, [[maharajikas]], [[Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitabha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

'''Tuṣita''' ([[Sanskrit]]) or '''Tusita''' ([[Pāli]]) is one of the six [[Deva (Buddhism)|deva]]-worlds of the [[Desire realm|Desire Realm]] (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[meditation]]. It is the heaven where the [[Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[gana]] deities: [[adityas]], [[visvedevas]], [[vasus]], tushitas, [[abhasvaras]], anilas, [[maharajikas]], [[Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitabha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

{{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] [[Bodhisattva]] in Tushita Heaven. [[Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[Nalanda]], [[Bihar]], [[India]]]]
'''Tuṣita''' ([[Sanskrit]]) or '''Tusita''' ([[Pāli]]) is one of the six [[Deva (Buddhism)|deva]]-worlds of the [[Desire realm|Desire Realm]] (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[meditation]]. It is the heaven where the [[Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[gana]] deities: [[adityas]], [[visvedevas]], [[vasus]], tushitas, [[abhasvaras]], anilas, [[maharajikas]], [[Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitabha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

[[Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[Nalanda]], [[Bihar]], [[India]]]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] [[Bodhisattva]] in Tushita Heaven. [[Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[Nalanda]], [[Bihar]], [[India]]]]
'''Tuṣita''' ([[Sanskrit]]) or '''Tusita''' ([[Pāli]]) is one of the six [[Deva (Buddhism)|deva]]-worlds of the [[Desire realm|Desire Realm]] (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[meditation]]. It is the heaven where the [[Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[gana]] deities: [[adityas]], [[visvedevas]], [[vasus]], tushitas, [[abhasvaras]], anilas, [[maharajikas]], [[Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitabha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.
== References == {{commonscat|Tushita heaven}} <references/>
]] ]]

{{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist cosmology]] [[Category:Pure Land Buddhism| ]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tushita]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Tushita]] [[en:Tushita]] [[en:Tushita [[sn:Tushita]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] [[Bodhisattva]] in Tushita Heaven. [[Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[Nalanda]], [[Bihar]], [[India]]]]
'''Tuṣita''' ([[Sanskrit]]) or '''Tusita''' ([[Pāli]]) is one of the six [[Deva (Buddhism)|deva]]-worlds of the [[Desire realm|Desire Realm]] (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[meditation]]. It is the heaven where the [[Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[gana]] deities: [[adityas]], [[visvedevas]], [[vasus]], tushitas, [[abhasvaras]], anilas, [[maharajikas]], [[Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitabha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.
== References == {{commonscat|Tushita heaven}} <references/> ]] ]] {{buddhism-stub}} {{cosmology-stub}}

{{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] [[Bodhisattva]] in Tushita Heaven. [[Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[Nalanda]], [[Bihar]], [[India]]]]
'''Tuṣita''' ([[Sanskrit]]) or '''Tusita''' ([[Pāli]]) is one of the six [[Deva (Buddhism)|deva]]-worlds of the [[Desire realm|Desire Realm]] (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[meditation]]. It is the heaven where the [[Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[gana]] deities: [[adityas]], [[visvedevas]], [[vasus]], tushitas, [[abhasvaras]], anilas, [[maharajikas]], [[Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.
== References == {{commonscat|Tushita heaven}} <references/> ]] ]] {{buddhism-stub}} {{cosmology-stub}}

[[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }}
[[sn:Tushita]] [[en:Tushita]] [[en:Tushita [[sn:Tushita]]
[[Category:Pages without translation templates]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[bn:তুষিত]] {{Commons category|Tushita heaven}} [[cs:Tusita]] [[de:Tushita]] [[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]] [[es:Tushita]] [[et:Tušita]] [[fa:توسیتا]] [[hu:Tusita]] [[id:Tusita]] [[it:Tuṣita]] [[ja:兜率天]] [[ko:도솔천]] [[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]] [[no:Tushita]] [[pl:Tuszita]] [[pt:Tushita]] [[ru:Тушита]] [[sl:Tušita]] [[ta:துசிதா]] [[th:ดุสิต]] [[uk:Тушіта]] [[zh:兜率天]]

Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

revision.text:
{{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] [[Bodhisattva]] in Tushita Heaven. [[Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[Nalanda]], [[Bihar]], [[India]]]]
'''Tuṣita''' ([[Sanskrit]]) or '''Tusita''' ([[Pāli]]) is one of the six [[Deva (Buddhism)|deva]]-worlds of the [[Desire realm|Desire Realm]] (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[meditation]]. It is the heaven where the [[Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[:en:Abhidharma|Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[gana]] deities: [[:en:Adityas|Adityas]], [[visvedevas]], [[vasus]], tushitas, [[:en:abhasvaras|abhasvaras]], anilas, [[maharajikas]], [[Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[Bodhisattvas]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[Pure Land]] of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.
== References == {{commonscat|Tushita heaven}} <references/> ]] ]] {{buddhism-stub}} {{cosmology-stub}}

Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[:en:Meditation|Meditation]]. Most Buddhist literature holds that [[:en:Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[:en:Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[:en:Abhidharma|Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[:en:Hinduism|Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[:en:Gana|Gana]] deities: [[:en:Adityas|Adityas]], [[:en:visvedevas|visvedevas]], [[:en:Vasus|Vasus]], tushitas, [[:en:abhasvaras|abhasvaras]], anilas, [[:en:maharajikas|maharajikas]], [[:en:Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[:en:Rudras|Rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[:en:Pali Canon|Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[:en:en:Larger Sutra|en:Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]] of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

[[Category:Pages without translation templates]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[bn:তুষিত]] {{Commons category|Tushita heaven}} [[cs:Tusita]] [[de:Tushita]] [[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]] [[es:Tushita]] [[et:Tušita]] [[fa:توسیتا]] [[hu:Tusita]] [[id:Tusita]] [[it:Tuṣita]] [[ja:兜率天]] [[ko:도솔천]] [[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]] [[no:Tushita]] [[pl:Tuszita]] [[pt:Tushita]] [[ru:Тушита]] [[sl:Tušita]] [[ta:துசிதா]] [[th:ดุสิต]] [[uk:Тушіта]] [[zh:兜率天]] {{wikidata link|Q660582}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[sn:commons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q660582]] Wikidata name: Tushita [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[:en:Kushan Dynasty|Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] in Tushita Heaven. [[:en:Palm leaf manuscript|Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[:en:Nalanda|Nalanda]], [[:en:Bihar|Bihar]], [[:en:India|India]]]]
'''Tuṣita''' ([[:en:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]]) or '''Tusita''' ([[:en:Pāli|Pāli]]) is one of the six [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deva]]-worlds of the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]] (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[:en:Meditation|Meditation]]. It is the heaven where the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[:en:Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[:en:Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[:en:Abhidharma|Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[:en:Hinduism|Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[:en:Gana|Gana]] deities: [[:en:Adityas|Adityas]], [[:en:visvedevas|visvedevas]], [[:en:Vasus|Vasus]], tushitas, [[:en:abhasvaras|abhasvaras]], anilas, [[:en:maharajikas|maharajikas]], [[:en:Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[:en:Rudras|Rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[:en:Pali Canon|Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[:en:en:Larger Sutra|en:Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]] of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>

Most Buddhist literature holds that [[:en:Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[:en:Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[:en:Abhidharma|Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[:en:Hinduism|Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[:en:Gana|Gana]] deities: [[:en:Adityas|Adityas]], [[:en:visvedevas|visvedevas]], [[:en:Vasus|Vasus]], tushitas, [[:en:abhasvaras|abhasvaras]], anilas, [[:en:maharajikas|maharajikas]], [[:en:Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[:en:Rudras|Rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[:en:Pali Canon|Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[:en:en:Larger Sutra|en:Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]] of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]] of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.
