<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[sn:commons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q660582]] Wikidata name: Tushita [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[:en:Kushan Dynasty|Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] in Tushita Heaven. [[:en:Palm leaf manuscript|Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[:en:Nalanda|Nalanda]], [[:en:Bihar|Bihar]], [[:en:India|India]]]]
'''Tuṣita''' ([[:en:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]]) or '''Tusita''' ([[:en:Pāli|Pāli]]) is one of the six [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deva]]-worlds of the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]] (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[:en:Meditation|Meditation]]. It is the heaven where the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[:en:Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[:en:Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[:en:Abhidharma|Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[:en:Hinduism|Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[:en:Gana|Gana]] deities: [[:en:Adityas|Adityas]], [[:en:visvedevas|visvedevas]], [[:en:Vasus|Vasus]], tushitas, [[:en:abhasvaras|abhasvaras]], anilas, [[:en:maharajikas|maharajikas]], [[:en:Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[:en:Rudras|Rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[:en:Pali Canon|Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[:en:en:Larger Sutra|en:Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]] of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[commons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[commons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[sn:commons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q660582]] Wikidata name: Tushita [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[:en:Kushan Dynasty|Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] in Tushita Heaven. [[:en:Palm leaf manuscript|Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[:en:Nalanda|Nalanda]], [[:en:Bihar|Bihar]], [[:en:India|India]]]]
'''Tuṣita''' ([[:en:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]]) or '''Tusita''' ([[:en:Pāli|Pāli]]) is one of the six [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deva]]-worlds of the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]] (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[:en:Meditation|Meditation]]. It is the heaven where the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[:en:Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[:en:Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[:en:Abhidharma|Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[:en:Hinduism|Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[:en:Gana|Gana]] deities: [[:en:Adityas|Adityas]], [[:en:visvedevas|visvedevas]], [[:en:Vasus|Vasus]], tushitas, [[:en:abhasvaras|abhasvaras]], anilas, [[:en:maharajikas|maharajikas]], [[:en:Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[:en:Rudras|Rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[:en:Pali Canon|Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[:en:en:Larger Sutra|en:Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]] of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]] of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[commons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[commons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[sn:commons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q660582]] Wikidata name: Tushita [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[:en:Kushan Dynasty|Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] in Tushita Heaven. [[:en:Palm leaf manuscript|Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[:en:Nalanda|Nalanda]], [[:en:Bihar|Bihar]], [[:en:India|India]]]]
'''Tuṣita''' ([[:en:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]]) or '''Tusita''' ([[:en:Pāli|Pāli]]) is one of the six [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deva]]-worlds of the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]] (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[:en:Meditation|Meditation]]. It is the heaven where the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[:en:Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[:en:Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[:en:Abhidharma|Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[:en:Hinduism|Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[:en:Gana|Gana]] deities: [[:en:Adityas|Adityas]], [[:en:visvedevas|visvedevas]], [[:en:Vasus|Vasus]], tushitas, [[:en:abhasvaras|abhasvaras]], anilas, [[:en:maharajikas|maharajikas]], [[:en:Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[:en:Rudras|Rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[:en:Pali Canon|Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[:en:en:Larger Sutra|en:Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]] of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[:en:en:Larger Sutra|en:Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]] of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

[[:en:Palm leaf manuscript|Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[:en:Nalanda|Nalanda]], [[:en:Bihar|Bihar]], [[:en:India|India]]]]
'''Tuṣita''' ([[:en:Sanskrit|Sanskrit]]) or '''Tusita''' ([[:en:Pāli|Pāli]]) is one of the six [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deva]]-worlds of the [[:en:Desire realm|Desire Realm]] (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through [[:en:Meditation|Meditation]]. It is the heaven where the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that [[:en:Maya (mother of the Buddha)|Queen Maya]] died seven days after the birth of her son [[:en:Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit [[:en:Trāyastriṃśa|Tavatimsa]] Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the [[:en:Abhidharma|Abhidharma]] to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In [[:en:Hinduism|Hinduism]], the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine [[:en:Gana|Gana]] deities: [[:en:Adityas|Adityas]], [[:en:visvedevas|visvedevas]], [[:en:Vasus|Vasus]], tushitas, [[:en:abhasvaras|abhasvaras]], anilas, [[:en:maharajikas|maharajikas]], [[:en:Sadhyas|sadhyas]], and the [[:en:Rudras|Rudras]].<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the [[:en:Pali Canon|Pali Canon]],<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:

{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the [[:en:Pure Land|Pure Land]] of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

Most Buddhist literature holds that {{ill|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|en|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|WD=Q877831|lt=Queen Maya}} died seven days after the birth of her son [[:en:Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit {{ill|Trāyastriṃśa|en|Trāyastriṃśa|WD=Q1360289|lt=Tavatimsa}} Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the {{ill|Abhidharma|en|Abhidharma|WD=Q612405|lt=Abhidharma}} to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In {{ill|Hinduism|en|Hinduism|WD=Q9089|lt=Hinduism}}, the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine {{ill|Gana|en|Gana|WD=Q632279|lt=Gana}} deities: {{ill|Adityas|en|Adityas|WD=Q357630|lt=Adityas}}, {{ill|visvedevas|en|visvedevas|WD=Q4112615|lt=visvedevas}}, [[:en:Vasus|Vasus]], tushitas, {{ill|abhasvaras|en|abhasvaras|WD=Q4055243|lt=abhasvaras}}, anilas, {{ill|maharajikas|en|maharajikas|WD=Q116756128|lt=maharajikas}}, {{ill|Sadhyas|en|Sadhyas|WD=Q116763819|lt=sadhyas}}, and the {{ill|Rudras|en|Rudras|WD=Q7377524|lt=Rudras}}.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the {{ill|Pali Canon|en|Pali Canon|WD=Q215685|lt=Pali Canon}},<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[:en:en:Larger Sutra|en:Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the {{ill|Pure Land|en|Pure Land|WD=Q732344|lt=Pure Land}} of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

[[:en:Palm leaf manuscript|Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[:en:Nalanda|Nalanda]], {{ill|Bihar|en|Bihar|WD=Q1165|lt=Bihar}}, {{ill|India|en|India|WD=Q668|lt=India}}]]
'''Tuṣita''' ({{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|WD=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit}}) or '''Tusita''' ([[:en:Pāli|Pāli]]) is one of the six {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=deva}}-worlds of the {{ill|Desire realm|en|Desire realm|WD=Q4447630|lt=Desire Realm}} (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through {{ill|Meditation|en|Meditation|WD=Q108458|lt=Meditation}}. It is the heaven where the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that {{ill|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|en|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|WD=Q877831|lt=Queen Maya}} died seven days after the birth of her son [[:en:Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit {{ill|Trāyastriṃśa|en|Trāyastriṃśa|WD=Q1360289|lt=Tavatimsa}} Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the {{ill|Abhidharma|en|Abhidharma|WD=Q612405|lt=Abhidharma}} to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In {{ill|Hinduism|en|Hinduism|WD=Q9089|lt=Hinduism}}, the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine {{ill|Gana|en|Gana|WD=Q632279|lt=Gana}} deities: {{ill|Adityas|en|Adityas|WD=Q357630|lt=Adityas}}, {{ill|visvedevas|en|visvedevas|WD=Q4112615|lt=visvedevas}}, [[:en:Vasus|Vasus]], tushitas, {{ill|abhasvaras|en|abhasvaras|WD=Q4055243|lt=abhasvaras}}, anilas, {{ill|maharajikas|en|maharajikas|WD=Q116756128|lt=maharajikas}}, {{ill|Sadhyas|en|Sadhyas|WD=Q116763819|lt=sadhyas}}, and the {{ill|Rudras|en|Rudras|WD=Q7377524|lt=Rudras}}.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>

Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the {{ill|Pali Canon|en|Pali Canon|WD=Q215685|lt=Pali Canon}},<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}

{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the {{ill|Pure Land|en|Pure Land|WD=Q732344|lt=Pure Land}} of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[co:commons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[co:commons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[sn:commons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q660582]] Wikidata name: Tushita [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[:en:Kushan Dynasty|Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} in Tushita Heaven. [[:en:Palm leaf manuscript|Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[:en:Nalanda|Nalanda]], {{ill|Bihar|en|Bihar|WD=Q1165|lt=Bihar}}, {{ill|India|en|India|WD=Q668|lt=India}}]]
'''Tuṣita''' ({{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|WD=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit}}) or '''Tusita''' ([[:en:Pāli|Pāli]]) is one of the six {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=deva}}-worlds of the {{ill|Desire realm|en|Desire realm|WD=Q4447630|lt=Desire Realm}} (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through {{ill|Meditation|en|Meditation|WD=Q108458|lt=Meditation}}. It is the heaven where the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that {{ill|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|en|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|WD=Q877831|lt=Queen Maya}} died seven days after the birth of her son [[:en:Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit {{ill|Trāyastriṃśa|en|Trāyastriṃśa|WD=Q1360289|lt=Tavatimsa}} Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the {{ill|Abhidharma|en|Abhidharma|WD=Q612405|lt=Abhidharma}} to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In {{ill|Hinduism|en|Hinduism|WD=Q9089|lt=Hinduism}}, the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine {{ill|Gana|en|Gana|WD=Q632279|lt=Gana}} deities: {{ill|Adityas|en|Adityas|WD=Q357630|lt=Adityas}}, {{ill|visvedevas|en|visvedevas|WD=Q4112615|lt=visvedevas}}, [[:en:Vasus|Vasus]], tushitas, {{ill|abhasvaras|en|abhasvaras|WD=Q4055243|lt=abhasvaras}}, anilas, {{ill|maharajikas|en|maharajikas|WD=Q116756128|lt=maharajikas}}, {{ill|Sadhyas|en|Sadhyas|WD=Q116763819|lt=sadhyas}}, and the {{ill|Rudras|en|Rudras|WD=Q7377524|lt=Rudras}}.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the {{ill|Pali Canon|en|Pali Canon|WD=Q215685|lt=Pali Canon}},<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[:en:en:Larger Sutra|en:Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the {{ill|Pure Land|en|Pure Land|WD=Q732344|lt=Pure Land}} of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.
== References == {{commonscat|Tushita heaven}} <references/>]] {{buddhism-stub}} {{cosmology-stub}}

In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the {{ill|Pali Canon|en|Pali Canon|WD=Q215685|lt=Pali Canon}},<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[:en:en:Larger Sutra|en:Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the {{ill|Pure Land|en|Pure Land|WD=Q732344|lt=Pure Land}} of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

{{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[:en:Kushan Dynasty|Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} in Tushita Heaven. [[:en:Palm leaf manuscript|Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[:en:Nalanda|Nalanda]], {{ill|Bihar|en|Bihar|WD=Q1165|lt=Bihar}}, {{ill|India|en|India|WD=Q668|lt=India}}]]
'''Tuṣita''' ({{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|WD=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit}}) or '''Tusita''' ([[:en:Pāli|Pāli]]) is one of the six {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=deva}}-worlds of the {{ill|Desire realm|en|Desire realm|WD=Q4447630|lt=Desire Realm}} (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through {{ill|Meditation|en|Meditation|WD=Q108458|lt=Meditation}}. It is the heaven where the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that {{ill|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|en|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|WD=Q877831|lt=Queen Maya}} died seven days after the birth of her son [[:en:Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit {{ill|Trāyastriṃśa|en|Trāyastriṃśa|WD=Q1360289|lt=Tavatimsa}} Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the {{ill|Abhidharma|en|Abhidharma|WD=Q612405|lt=Abhidharma}} to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In {{ill|Hinduism|en|Hinduism|WD=Q9089|lt=Hinduism}}, the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine {{ill|Gana|en|Gana|WD=Q632279|lt=Gana}} deities: {{ill|Adityas|en|Adityas|WD=Q357630|lt=Adityas}}, {{ill|visvedevas|en|visvedevas|WD=Q4112615|lt=visvedevas}}, [[:en:Vasus|Vasus]], tushitas, {{ill|abhasvaras|en|abhasvaras|WD=Q4055243|lt=abhasvaras}}, anilas, {{ill|maharajikas|en|maharajikas|WD=Q116756128|lt=maharajikas}}, {{ill|Sadhyas|en|Sadhyas|WD=Q116763819|lt=sadhyas}}, and the {{ill|Rudras|en|Rudras|WD=Q7377524|lt=Rudras}}.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>

{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}

Other {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the {{ill|Pure Land|en|Pure Land|WD=Q732344|lt=Pure Land}} of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[co:commons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[co:commons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[sn:commons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]]
{{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[:en:Kushan Dynasty|Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} in Tushita Heaven. [[:en:Palm leaf manuscript|Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[:en:Nalanda|Nalanda]], {{ill|Bihar|en|Bihar|WD=Q1165|lt=Bihar}}, {{ill|India|en|India|WD=Q668|lt=India}}]]
'''Tuṣita''' ({{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|WD=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit}}) or '''Tusita''' ([[:en:Pāli|Pāli]]) is one of the six {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=deva}}-worlds of the {{ill|Desire realm|en|Desire realm|WD=Q4447630|lt=Desire Realm}} (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through {{ill|Meditation|en|Meditation|WD=Q108458|lt=Meditation}}. It is the heaven where the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that {{ill|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|en|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|WD=Q877831|lt=Queen Maya}} died seven days after the birth of her son [[:en:Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit {{ill|Trāyastriṃśa|en|Trāyastriṃśa|WD=Q1360289|lt=Tavatimsa}} Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the {{ill|Abhidharma|en|Abhidharma|WD=Q612405|lt=Abhidharma}} to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In {{ill|Hinduism|en|Hinduism|WD=Q9089|lt=Hinduism}}, the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine {{ill|Gana|en|Gana|WD=Q632279|lt=Gana}} deities: {{ill|Adityas|en|Adityas|WD=Q357630|lt=Adityas}}, {{ill|visvedevas|en|visvedevas|WD=Q4112615|lt=visvedevas}}, [[:en:Vasus|Vasus]], tushitas, {{ill|abhasvaras|en|abhasvaras|WD=Q4055243|lt=abhasvaras}}, anilas, {{ill|maharajikas|en|maharajikas|WD=Q116756128|lt=maharajikas}}, {{ill|Sadhyas|en|Sadhyas|WD=Q116763819|lt=sadhyas}}, and the {{ill|Rudras|en|Rudras|WD=Q7377524|lt=Rudras}}.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the {{ill|Pali Canon|en|Pali Canon|WD=Q215685|lt=Pali Canon}},<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[:en:en:Larger Sutra|en:Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the {{ill|Pure Land|en|Pure Land|WD=Q732344|lt=Pure Land}} of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.
== References == {{commonscat|Tushita heaven}} <references/>]] {{buddhism-stub}} {{cosmology-stub}}

In {{ill|Hinduism|en|Hinduism|WD=Q9089|lt=Hinduism|1=Hinduism}}, the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine {{ill|Gana|en|Gana|WD=Q632279|lt=Gana|1=Gana}} deities: {{ill|Adityas|en|Adityas|WD=Q357630|lt=Adityas|1=Adityas}}, {{ill|visvedevas|en|visvedevas|WD=Q4112615|lt=visvedevas|1=Visvedevas}}, [[:en:Vasus|Vasus]], tushitas, {{ill|abhasvaras|en|abhasvaras|WD=Q4055243|lt=abhasvaras|1=Abhasvaras}}, anilas, {{ill|maharajikas|en|maharajikas|WD=Q116756128|lt=maharajikas|1=Maharajikas}}, {{ill|Sadhyas|en|Sadhyas|WD=Q116763819|lt=sadhyas|1=Sadhyas}}, and the {{ill|Rudras|en|Rudras|WD=Q7377524|lt=Rudras|1=Rudras}}.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the {{ill|Pali Canon|en|Pali Canon|WD=Q215685|lt=Pali Canon|1=Pali Canon}},<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[:en:en:Larger Sutra|en:Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva|1=Bodhisattva}} dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the {{ill|Pure Land|en|Pure Land|WD=Q732344|lt=Pure Land|1=Pure Land}} of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

[[:en:Palm leaf manuscript|Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[:en:Nalanda|Nalanda]], {{ill|Bihar|en|Bihar|WD=Q1165|lt=Bihar|1=Bihar}}, {{ill|India|en|India|WD=Q668|lt=India|1=India}}]]
'''Tuṣita''' ({{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|WD=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit|1=Sanskrit}}) or '''Tusita''' ([[:en:Pāli|Pāli]]) is one of the six {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=deva|1=Deva (Buddhism)}}-worlds of the {{ill|Desire realm|en|Desire realm|WD=Q4447630|lt=Desire Realm|1=Desire realm}} (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through {{ill|Meditation|en|Meditation|WD=Q108458|lt=Meditation|1=Meditation}}. It is the heaven where the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva|1=Bodhisattva}} Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that {{ill|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|en|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|WD=Q877831|lt=Queen Maya|1=Maya (mother of the Buddha)}} died seven days after the birth of her son [[:en:Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit {{ill|Trāyastriṃśa|en|Trāyastriṃśa|WD=Q1360289|lt=Tavatimsa|1=Trāyastriṃśa}} Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the {{ill|Abhidharma|en|Abhidharma|WD=Q612405|lt=Abhidharma|1=Abhidharma}} to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In {{ill|Hinduism|en|Hinduism|WD=Q9089|lt=Hinduism|1=Hinduism}}, the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine {{ill|Gana|en|Gana|WD=Q632279|lt=Gana|1=Gana}} deities: {{ill|Adityas|en|Adityas|WD=Q357630|lt=Adityas|1=Adityas}}, {{ill|visvedevas|en|visvedevas|WD=Q4112615|lt=visvedevas|1=Visvedevas}}, [[:en:Vasus|Vasus]], tushitas, {{ill|abhasvaras|en|abhasvaras|WD=Q4055243|lt=abhasvaras|1=Abhasvaras}}, anilas, {{ill|maharajikas|en|maharajikas|WD=Q116756128|lt=maharajikas|1=Maharajikas}}, {{ill|Sadhyas|en|Sadhyas|WD=Q116763819|lt=sadhyas|1=Sadhyas}}, and the {{ill|Rudras|en|Rudras|WD=Q7377524|lt=Rudras|1=Rudras}}.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the {{ill|Pali Canon|en|Pali Canon|WD=Q215685|lt=Pali Canon|1=Pali Canon}},<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[:en:en:Larger Sutra|en:Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva|1=Bodhisattva}} dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the {{ill|Pure Land|en|Pure Land|WD=Q732344|lt=Pure Land|1=Pure Land}} of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[co:commons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[co:commons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bn:তুষিত]][[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tushita heaven]][[cs:Tusita]][[de:Tushita]][[en:Tushita]][[eo:Paradizo de la kontentaj dioj]][[es:Tushita]][[et:Tušita]][[fa:توسیتا]][[hu:Tusita]][[id:Tusita]][[it:Tuṣita]][[ja:兜率天]][[ko:도솔천]][[my:တုသိတာဘုံ]][[no:Tushita]][[pl:Tuszita]][[pt:Tushita]][[ru:Тушита]][[sl:Tušita]][[ta:துசிதா]][[th:ดุสิต]][[uk:Тушіта]][[uz:Tushita]][[zh:兜率天]]
{{Short description|Term in Buddhism and Hinduism}} [[File:Tushita heaven - stone relief carving - pakistan.jpg|thumb|Stone relief carving of Tushita Heaven, carved during the [[:en:Kushan Dynasty|Kushan Dynasty]]]] [[File:Astasahasrika Prajnaparamita Maitreya Detail.jpeg|thumb|[[Maitreya]] {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva|1=Bodhisattva}} in Tushita Heaven. [[:en:Palm leaf manuscript|Palm leaf manuscript]]. [[:en:Nalanda|Nalanda]], {{ill|Bihar|en|Bihar|WD=Q1165|lt=Bihar|1=Bihar}}, {{ill|India|en|India|WD=Q668|lt=India|1=India}}]]
'''Tuṣita''' ({{ill|Sanskrit|en|Sanskrit|WD=Q11059|lt=Sanskrit|1=Sanskrit}}) or '''Tusita''' ([[:en:Pāli|Pāli]]) is one of the six {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=deva|1=Deva (Buddhism)}}-worlds of the {{ill|Desire realm|en|Desire realm|WD=Q4447630|lt=Desire Realm|1=Desire realm}} (Kāmadhātu), located between the Yāma heaven and the {{IAST|Nirmāṇarati}} heaven. Like the other heavens, {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is said to be reachable through {{ill|Meditation|en|Meditation|WD=Q108458|lt=Meditation|1=Meditation}}. It is the heaven where the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva|1=Bodhisattva}} Śvetaketu (Pāli: Setaketu, "White Banner") resided before being reborn on Earth as [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Gautama]], the historical Buddha; it is, likewise, the heaven where the Bodhisattva Nātha ("Protector") currently resides, who will later be born as the next Buddha, [[Maitreya]].
Most Buddhist literature holds that {{ill|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|en|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|WD=Q877831|lt=Queen Maya|1=Maya (mother of the Buddha)}} died seven days after the birth of her son [[:en:Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven. Seven years after the Buddha's enlightenment, she came down to visit {{ill|Trāyastriṃśa|en|Trāyastriṃśa|WD=Q1360289|lt=Tavatimsa|1=Trāyastriṃśa}} Heaven, where the Buddha later preached the {{ill|Abhidharma|en|Abhidharma|WD=Q612405|lt=Abhidharma|1=Abhidharma}} to her.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.palikanon.com/english/pali_names/maha/mahamaayaa.htm|title=Māyā|website=www.palikanon.com|access-date=2018-04-07}}</ref>
In {{ill|Hinduism|en|Hinduism|WD=Q9089|lt=Hinduism|1=Hinduism}}, the tushitas are referred to as one of the nine {{ill|Gana|en|Gana|WD=Q632279|lt=Gana|1=Gana}} deities: {{ill|Adityas|en|Adityas|WD=Q357630|lt=Adityas|1=Adityas}}, {{ill|visvedevas|en|visvedevas|WD=Q4112615|lt=visvedevas|1=Visvedevas}}, [[:en:Vasus|Vasus]], tushitas, {{ill|abhasvaras|en|abhasvaras|WD=Q4055243|lt=abhasvaras|1=Abhasvaras}}, anilas, {{ill|maharajikas|en|maharajikas|WD=Q116756128|lt=maharajikas|1=Maharajikas}}, {{ill|Sadhyas|en|Sadhyas|WD=Q116763819|lt=sadhyas|1=Sadhyas}}, and the {{ill|Rudras|en|Rudras|WD=Q7377524|lt=Rudras|1=Rudras}}.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Walker |first=Benjamin |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=6zj3DwAAQBAJ&dq=abhasvara+gana&pg=PA397 |title=Hindu World: An Encyclopedic Survey of Hinduism. In Two Volumes. Volume I A-L |date=2019-04-09 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-0-429-62465-0 |pages=397 |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Danielou |first=Alain |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=OIXtDwAAQBAJ&dq=%C4%80bh%C4%81svaras&pg=PA302 |title=The Myths and Gods of India: The Classic Work on Hindu Polytheism |date=2017-01-01 |publisher=Motilal Banarsidass |isbn=978-81-208-3638-9 |pages=302 |language=en}}</ref>
== Descriptions ==
Like all heaven realms in Buddhism, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is the residence of divine beings or ''devas''. According to the ''Visakhuposatha Sutta'' of the {{ill|Pali Canon|en|Pali Canon|WD=Q215685|lt=Pali Canon|1=Pali Canon}},<ref>[http://www.accesstoinsight.org/tipitaka/an/an08/an08.043.khan.html Visakhuposatha Sutta] (AN 8.43)</ref> time runs much differently than on Earth:
{{quote|That which among men is four hundred years, Visakha, is one night and day of the Tusita devas, their month has thirty of those days, their year twelve of those months; the lifespan of the Tusita devas is four thousand of those heavenly years...}}
==Mahayana View== In Mahayana Buddhist thought, the {{IAST|Tuṣita}} Heaven is where all Bodhisattvas destined to reach full enlightenment in their next life dwell for a time. One such reference can be found in the [[:en:en:Larger Sutra|en:Larger Sutra]] of Immeasurable Life, a Mahayana text:
{{quote|Each of these bodhisattvas, following the virtues of the Mahasattva Samantabhadra, is endowed with the immeasurable practices and vows of the Bodhisattva Path, and firmly dwells in all the meritorious deeds. He freely travels in all the ten quarters and employs skillful means of emancipation. He enters the treasury of the Dharma of the Buddhas, and reaches the Other Shore. Throughout the innumerable worlds he attains Enlightenment. First, dwelling in the Tusita Heaven, he proclaims the true Dharma. Having left the heavenly palace, he descends into his mother's womb.}}
The {{IAST|Tuṣita}} heaven is therefore closely associated with Maitreya, and many Buddhists vow to be reborn there so that they can hear the teachings of the Bodhisattva and ultimately be reborn with him when he becomes a Buddha. Other {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva|1=Bodhisattva}} dwell in this heaven realm from time to time. {{IAST|Tuṣita}} is part of the same world-system as Earth, and so is relatively close, whereas the {{ill|Pure Land|en|Pure Land|WD=Q732344|lt=Pure Land|1=Pure Land}} of [[Amitābha]] Buddha is treated as a separate world-system entirely.
== References == {{commonscat|Tushita heaven}} <references/>]] {{buddhism-stub}} {{cosmology-stub}}

Most Buddhist literature holds that {{ill|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|en|Maya (mother of the Buddha)|WD=Q877831|lt=Queen Maya|1=Maya (mother of the Buddha)}} died seven days after the birth of her son [[:en:Gautama Buddha|the Buddha]], and was then reborn in the Tushita Heaven.
